content: Proper wound care after surgery is essential for healing and preventing infection. Follow these guidelines: 1) Keep the wound clean and dry - avoid getting the incision wet for the first 48 hours unless instructed otherwise. After that, gently clean with mild soap and water. 2) Dressing changes - change dressings as instructed by your healthcare provider, typically once or twice daily. Wash hands thoroughly before and after. 3) Signs of infection - monitor for redness spreading from the incision, increased warmth, swelling, pus or drainage, fever above 100.4°F, or increasing pain. Contact your provider immediately if these occur. 4) Activity restrictions - avoid heavy lifting, strenuous activity, or stretching the incision area as directed. 5) Suture or staple removal - attend your scheduled appointment for removal, typically 7-14 days after surgery. 6) Scar management - once healed, protect from sun exposure and consider silicone sheets or gel as recommended.

citationFormat.citationText: Wound Healing Society. (2023). Post-Surgical Wound Care Guidelines. Retrieved from https://woundheal.org/guidelines/surgical-wound-care
